fields.content: 🌿 Sandhivata (Vataja degeneration) must be distinguished from Amavata (inflammatory — Ama + Vata). Key difference: Amavata = morning stiffness >30 min + tender warm joints + systemic features + positive RA factor. Sandhivata = mechanical pain, crepitus, worse on activity, improved by rest.

⚠ Red flags requiring urgent referral: Night pain + weight loss (malignancy). Saddle anaesthesia + bladder changes (Cauda Equina — surgical emergency). Fever + severe back pain (spinal infection). Wrist drop/foot drop (nerve compression).

fields.label: Samprapti / Treatment Notes
fields.placeholder: Sroto involved, Sthana Samshraya, PK suitability notes…

fields.label: X-Ray Findings
fields.placeholder: e.g. Joint space narrowing, osteophytes, disc collapse

fields.label: MRI / CT Findings
fields.placeholder: e.g. L4-L5 disc prolapse, PIVD, cord compression

steps.title: Gait & Posture (Chankramana Pariksha)
items: Antalgic gait — lean away from painful side
Trendelenburg sign — hip abductor weakness (hip OA)
Waddling gait — bilateral hip disease
Steppage gait — foot drop (L4-L5 root compression)
Posture — kyphosis, scoliosis, loss of lumbar lordosis

steps.title: Inspection of Joint / Spine
items: Swelling — bony (osteophytes) vs soft tissue vs effusion
Deformity — varus/valgus (knee), fixed flexion, subluxation
Muscle wasting — quadriceps (knee OA), thenar/hypothenar (cervical myelopathy)
Skin changes over joint — erythema (acute inflammatory), psoriatic plaques

steps.title: Palpation (Sparsha Pariksha)
items: Joint line tenderness (medial/lateral meniscus — knee)
Spinous process tenderness — level of pathology
Paraspinal muscle spasm / trigger points
Effusion test — patella tap / bulge sign (knee)
Temperature — warm = active inflammation (Saama / Pittaja)

steps.title: Range of Motion
items: Schober test — lumbar flexion (mark L5+10cm, flexion <15cm = restricted)
Cervical ROM — flexion 45°, extension 45°, lateral 45°, rotation 60°
Knee — full extension (0°) to flexion (135°)
Hip — flexion 120°, abduction 45°, internal/external rotation 45°
Always compare bilateral; note pain at end-range vs throughout

steps.title: Neurological Assessment
items: Motor power — MRC grading 0–5 in relevant myotomes
Reflexes — patellar (L3-L4), Achilles (S1), supinator (C5-C6)
Sensation — light touch, pin-prick along dermatomes
Upper motor neuron signs — Babinski, clonus (spinal cord compression)

steps.title: Special Tests (site-specific)
items: SLR (Lasègue) — L4/L5/S1 root; positive <60° (Gridhrasi)
Bragard — SLR + dorsiflexion (increases radicular pain)
Spurling — cervical compression (cervical radiculopathy)
FABER/Patrick — hip pathology (groin pain)
Thomas test — fixed flexion deformity of hip
McMurray test — meniscal tear (knee)

reference_values.abnormal: >9 or gout attack at any level
